title: Field-Induced Local Excitations Causing Zero-Magnetization Plateaus in Antiferromagnets of Antiferromagnetic Spin Dimers Under Magnetic Field
authors: Myung-Hwan Whangbo, Hyun-Joo Koo, Nikita V. Astakhov, Peter S. Berdonosov, Olga S. Volkova
abstract: Certain antiferromagnets composed of antiferromagnetic spin dimers exhibit a zero-magnetization plateau despite that the single-ion anisotropy of their magnetic ions is negligible. The cause for this observation was investigated by analyzing how a magnetic field affects the energy spectrum of an antiferromagnetic chain composed of antiferromagnetic spin dimers made up of two spin-half ions and by carrying out specific heat measurements for potassium copper chloride as a function magnetic field at 2 K.
summary: This paper investigates why certain antiferromagnets maintain a zero-magnetization plateau under a magnetic field despite having negligible single-ion anisotropy. It identifies field-induced triplet excitations as the driving mechanism and explains the observed asymmetry in specific heat through the increasing energy cost of breaking interdimer bonds.
main_problem: Explaining the mechanism behind zero-magnetization plateaus in antiferromagnets composed of spin dimers where single-ion anisotropy is negligible.
main_result: The study proposes that field-induced local excitations (mixing of the triplet state into the singlet ground state) drive the plateau, and that the asymmetry in specific heat peaks arises from increasing energy requirements for subsequent spin-flips as interdimer bonds break.
method: The authors combine experimental specific heat and magnetization measurements on KCuCl3 with theoretical analysis involving DFT+U calculations, energy-spectrum modeling, and statistical analysis using binomial coefficients.
model_or_system: The system consists of antiferromagnetic spin dimers in molecular solids KCuCl3 and TlCuCl3, modeled as an AFM chain of spin-1/2 dimers with intradimer exchange J and interdimer exchange J'.
key_observables: Magnetization (M/M_sat), specific heat (Cp), magnetic entropy (Sm), and the energy gap (Delta).
important_parameters: Intradimer exchange (J), interdimer exchange (J'), magnetic field (H), and temperature (T).
main_assumptions: Assumes the phonon contribution to specific heat decreases linearly with the magnetic field and uses a binomial distribution to describe the configuration of magnetic species.
figures_summary: Figure 1 shows Type I plateau behavior (spin-flop and magnetization); Figure 2 illustrates the Type II plateau process in an AFM chain; Figure 3 shows singlet/triplet states; Figure 7 shows the asymmetric field dependence of specific heat.
paper_structure: The paper begins by defining Type I and Type II plateaus, proposes a mechanism for energy absorption via local excitations, compares KCuCl3 and TlCuCl3, analyzes the specific heat asymmetry through a statistical model of bond-breaking, and concludes with a discussion on spin orientation via spin-orbit coupling.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks
